name: qwdb

metadata.name: qwdb
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU General Public License v3 (GPLv3)
metadata.summary: An improbable web debugger through WebSockets (client only)

documentation: See http://github.com/quilsoft-org/wdb

methods.name: wdb.state.State.up
documentation: Go up in stack and return True if top frame

classes.name: wdb.state.Running
documentation: Running state: never stopping

classes.name: wdb.state.Step
documentation: Stepping state: always stopping

classes.name: wdb.state.Next
documentation: Nexting state: stop if same frame

classes.name: wdb.state.Until
documentation: Nexting until state: stop if same frame and is next line

classes.name: wdb.state.Return
documentation: Returning state: Stop on return event if same frame

classes.name: wdb.breakpoint.Breakpoint
documentation: Simple breakpoint that breaks if in file

classes.name: wdb.breakpoint.LineBreakpoint
documentation: Simple breakpoint that breaks if in file at line

classes.name: wdb.breakpoint.ConditionalBreakpoint
documentation: Breakpoint that breaks if condition is True at line in file

classes.name: wdb.breakpoint.FunctionBreakpoint
documentation: Breakpoint that breaks if in file in function

functions.name: wdb.utils.inplace
documentation:
Allow for a file to be replaced with new content.

yields a tuple of (readable, writable) file objects, where writable
replaces readable.

If an exception occurs, the old file is restored, removing the
written data.

mode should *not* use 'w', 'a' or '+'; only read-only-modes are supported.
Got from:
http://www.zopatista.com/python/2013/11/26/inplace-file-rewriting/
as suggested by https://github.com/leorochael

methods.name: wdb.utils.Html5Diff._format_line
documentation:
Returns HTML markup of "from" / "to" text lines

side -- 0 or 1 indicating "from" or "to" text
flag -- indicates if difference on line
linenum -- line number (used for line number column)
text -- line text to be marked up

methods.name: wdb.utils.Html5Diff.make_table
documentation:
Returns HTML table of side by side comparison with change highlights

Arguments:
fromlines -- list of "from" lines
tolines -- list of "to" lines
fromdesc -- "from" file column header string
todesc -- "to" file column header string
context -- set to True for contextual differences (defaults to False
    which shows full differences).
numlines -- number of context lines.  When context is set True,
    controls number of lines displayed before and after the change.
    When context is False, controls the number of lines to place
    the "next" link anchors before the next change (so click of
    "next" link jumps to just before the change).

functions.name: wdb._compat.wait
documentation:
Wait till an object in object_list is ready/readable.
Returns list of those objects in object_list which are
ready/readable.

functions.name: wdb._compat.wait

This method is called when there is the remote possibility

This function is called if an exception occurs,
